name: Basic Nmap Scan
description: Performs a default scan of the top 1000 TCP ports on the target. This is the simplest way to discover open services.

arguments.<target>: IP address, hostname, or CIDR range to scan

expected_output:
PORT     STATE SERVICE
22/tcp   open  ssh
80/tcp   open  http
443/tcp  open  https
use_case: Quick initial scan to identify what services are running on a target.
common_mistakes: Not checking if host is up first

name: Full Service Version Scan
description: Comprehensive scan: detects service versions (-sV), runs default scripts (-sC), and scans all 65535 TCP ports (-p-). This is the go-to scan for thorough enumeration.

arguments.-sV: Probe open ports to determine service/version info

expected_output:
PORT     STATE SERVICE  VERSION
22/tcp   open  ssh      OpenSSH 8.9p1
80/tcp   open  http     Apache httpd 2.4.52
3306/tcp open  mysql    MySQL 8.0.32
use_case: Thorough reconnaissance to identify all services, their exact versions, and gather additional info via scripts.
prerequisites: Sufficient time (full port scan is slow)
detections: IDS alerts for sequential port scanning
Firewall logs showing connection attempts to all ports
remediation: Use port knocking for sensitive services
common_mistakes: Running without -Pn when host appears down

name: UDP Port Scan
description: Scans the top 100 UDP ports. UDP scanning is slower than TCP but critical — services like DNS (53), SNMP (161), TFTP (69), and NTP (123) run on UDP.

arguments.--top-ports 100: Scan only the 100 most common UDP ports

expected_output:
PORT    STATE         SERVICE
53/udp  open          domain
161/udp open          snmp
123/udp open          ntp
use_case: Discover UDP services that TCP scans miss. SNMP and DNS are common attack vectors.
detections: Unusual UDP traffic patterns in flow data

name: OS Detection
description: Attempts to determine the operating system of the target by analyzing TCP/IP stack fingerprints.

arguments.--osscan-guess: Guess OS more aggressively if exact match not found

expected_output:
OS details: Linux 5.4 - 5.15
Network Distance: 1 hop
use_case: Identify the target OS to select appropriate exploits and enumeration techniques.
prerequisites: At least one open and one closed port on target
detections: Unusual TCP flag combinations in network traffic
common_mistakes: Target behind NAT may give inaccurate results

name: Stealth SYN Scan
description: Performs a SYN scan (half-open) with slower timing (-T2) and packet fragmentation (-f) to evade basic IDS/firewall detection.

arguments.-sS: SYN scan (half-open, doesn't complete TCP handshake)
arguments.-f: Fragment packets to evade simple packet filters

expected_output:
PORT   STATE SERVICE
22/tcp open  ssh
80/tcp open  http
use_case: Scan targets with IDS/IPS that might detect aggressive scanning. Useful for stealthy reconnaissance.
remediation: Use behavioral detection for slow scans

name: Vulnerability Script Scan
description: Runs all NSE scripts in the 'vuln' category to check for known vulnerabilities like EternalBlue, Heartbleed, ShellShock, etc.
expected_output:
| smb-vuln-ms17-010:
|   VULNERABLE:
|   Remote Code Execution vulnerability in Microsoft SMBv1
|     State: VULNERABLE
use_case: Quick vulnerability check against known CVEs without a full vulnerability scanner.
common_mistakes: Not combining with -sV for better results

name: Subdomain Enumeration
description: Discovers subdomains of a target domain using passive sources (certificate transparency, DNS datasets, search engines). Fast and non-intrusive.
use_case: Map the attack surface by discovering all subdomains. Often reveals forgotten dev/staging environments.
detections: Passive — no direct interaction with target
remediation: Monitor CT logs for unauthorized certificates
common_mistakes: Not combining with other tools for completeness

name: Amass Passive Enumeration
description: Comprehensive subdomain enumeration using 50+ data sources. Passive mode doesn't touch the target directly.

arguments.-passive: Only use passive sources (no DNS brute-force)

use_case: Most thorough passive subdomain discovery. Combines CT logs, DNS datasets, web archives, and more.

name: Email & Subdomain Harvesting
description: Gathers emails, subdomains, hosts, and employee names from public sources like search engines, PGP servers, and Shodan.
expected_output:
[*] Emails found:
  admin@example.com
  hr@example.com
[*] Hosts found:
  mail.example.com
  vpn.example.com
use_case: Gather email addresses for phishing campaigns and discover infrastructure through OSINT.
detections: Passive — queries search engines, not the target directly
remediation: Limit email exposure on public websites
common_mistakes: Not verifying found emails are still active

name: Fast Full Port Scan
description: Scans all 65535 ports at high speed. Masscan can scan the entire internet in under 6 minutes. Use for rapid port discovery before detailed nmap scan.

arguments.--rate=1000: Packets per second (adjust based on network)

use_case: Rapid port discovery on large networks. Follow up with nmap -sV on discovered ports.
common_mistakes: Setting rate too high (causes network issues)
Not following up with nmap for service details
